2603.20678.title: AI-Driven Multi-Agent Simulation of Stratified Polyamory Systems: A Computational Framework for Optimizing Social Reproductive Efficiency
2603.20678.paper_summary: Contemporary societies face a severe crisis of demographic reproduction. Global fertility rates continue to decline precipitously, with East Asian nations exhibiting the most dramatic trends -- China's total fertility rate (TFR) fell to approximately 1.0 in 2023, while South Korea's dropped below 0.72. Simultaneously, the institution of marriage is undergoing structural disintegration: educated women rationally reject unions lacking both emotional fulfillment and economic security, while a growing proportion of men at the lower end of the socioeconomic spectrum experience chronic sexual deprivation, anxiety, and learned helplessness. This paper proposes a computational framework for modeling and evaluating a Stratified Polyamory System (SPS) using techniques from agent-based modeling (ABM), multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), and large language model (LLM)-empowered social simulation. The SPS permits individuals to maintain a limited number of legally recognized secondary partners in addition to one primary spouse, combined with socialized child-rearing and inheritance reform. We formalize the A/B/C stratification as heterogeneous agent types in a multi-agent system and model the matching process as a MARL problem amenable to Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). The mating network is analyzed using graph neural network (GNN) representations. Drawing on evolutionary psychology, behavioral ecology, social stratification theory, computational social science, algorithmic fairness, and institutional economics, we argue that SPS can improve aggregate social welfare in the Pareto sense. Preliminary computational results demonstrate the framework's viability in addressing the dual crisis of female motherhood penalties and male sexlessness, while offering a non-violent mechanism for wealth dispersion analogous to the historical Chinese Grace Decree (Tui'en Ling).
2603.20678.paper_summary_zh: 當代社會面臨嚴重的人口再生危機。全球生育率持續急劇下降，東亞國家顯示出最明顯的趨勢——中國的總生育率（TFR）在2023年降至約1.0，而南韓則降至0.72以下。與此同時，婚姻制度正在經歷結構性解體：受過教育的女性理性地拒絕缺乏情感滿足和經濟安全的聯盟，而社會經濟光譜下端的男性則越來越多地經歷慢性的性剝奪、焦慮和習得的無助。本文提出了一個計算框架，用於建模和評估分層多元戀愛系統（SPS），使用來自基於代理的建模（ABM）、多代理強化學習（MARL）和大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的社會模擬的技術。SPS允許個體在擁有一名主要配偶的同時，維持有限數量的法律認可的次要伴侶，並結合社會化的育兒和繼承改革。我們將A/B/C分層形式化為多代理系統中的異質代理類型，並將匹配過程建模為適合於近端策略優化（PPO）的MARL問題。交配網絡使用圖神經網絡（GNN）表示進行分析。基於進化心理學、行為生態學、社會分層理論、計算社會科學、算法公平性和制度經濟學，我們認為SPS可以在帕累托意義上改善整體社會福利。初步計算結果顯示該框架在解決女性母親懲罰和男性無性問題的雙重危機方面的可行性，同時提供了一種非暴力的財富分配機制，類似於歷史上的中國恩典法令（Tui'en Ling）。
2603.20678.author: Yicai Xing et.al.
